items.q: Why are tropical rainforest soils typically infertile?
items.correct: Heavy rainfall leaches nutrients, and most nutrients are stored in living biomass and rapidly recycled — the soil itself holds very little.
items.misconception: Because so much vegetation grows there, the soil must be fertile.
items.random_wrong: Because rainforest soils are made of clay.
items.why: The lush vegetation hides poor soil. The rainforest works as a tightly closed nutrient cycle; once cleared, the system collapses quickly because the soil cannot sustain growth.

items.q: What is the difference between biotic and abiotic components of an ecosystem?
items.correct: Biotic components are all living organisms (plants, animals, microbes); abiotic components are non-living factors (climate, soil, water, light, rock).
items.misconception: Biotic means animals and abiotic means plants.
items.random_wrong: Biotic and abiotic both refer to types of soil.
items.why: Plants are biotic, not abiotic. Students sort by familiarity rather than living vs non-living. The distinction matters because abiotic factors set the conditions for which biotic communities develop.

items.correct: Development that meets the needs of the present without compromising the ability of future generations to meet their own needs — balancing economic, social and environmental goals.
items.misconception: Sustainable development means stopping all economic growth.
items.random_wrong: Sustainable development means using only solar power.
items.why: Students treat sustainability as anti-development. The Brundtland definition is about balance and intergenerational equity — not zero growth or one specific technology.

items.q: Which is an ABIOTIC factor in a pond?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Water temperature. “Tadpoles” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: What is the role of a DECOMPOSER in an ecosystem?
items.correct: Breaks down dead matter and recycles nutrients
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Breaks down dead matter and recycles nutrients. “Makes food from sunlight” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: What approximate percentage of energy is LOST between trophic levels in a food chain?
items.why: The real figure is 90. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
